method_atom: An exact-evaluation scheme for a global quantity defined as a product of local multi-index elements attached to the nodes of a network and coupled by pairwise summation along its links, where each local element satisfies a system of quadratic consistency constraints that lets the whole sum be recast as a Gaussian integral over anticommuting (Grassmann) variables. The evaluation is polynomial-time when the coupling network is topologically planar (genus zero); for a network of genus g it runs in time scaling as 2^{2g} times a low-degree polynomial, plus a correction governed by the minimum number of links one must delete to render the network planar.
object_atom: Matchgate tensor networks: products of tensors placed on the vertices of a graph and contracted over the indices shared along its edges, with each tensor obeying the matchgate (quadratic) identities, considered specifically on non-planar graphs of nonzero genus.
brief_reason: The Grassmann/Gaussian-integral summation technique with genus-controlled complexity is the transferable machinery, while the matchgate tensor networks defined on graphs are the domain-specific object; the two are cleanly separable.
